data.summary: This React component, StockChart, receives stock data as a prop and renders a responsive line chart using Recharts to visualize the historical closing prices of a company, displaying the company name, ticker, and interactive chart features such as tooltips and legends.

data.summary: This code defines an Express route handler for GET requests to "/api/stock/:symbol" that responds with either mock or real historical daily stock data (including date, close price, and volume) for the specified symbol, handling errors and formatting the response with the ticker, company name, and data array.

data.summary: This code defines an Express route handler for GET requests to "/api/stock/:symbol" that responds with either mock or real historical daily stock data (including date, close price, and volume) for the specified symbol, handling errors and formatting the response with the ticker, company name, and data array.

data.instruction: make it horizontal red line which shows the current stock price

data.summary: This code sets up three Express endpoints: a "/api/hello" GET route that responds with a simple greeting message, a "/api/stock/:symbol" GET route that returns either mock or real stock data (including ticker, name, historical daily data, and current price) for a given symbol depending on the USE_MOCK flag, and starts the server listening on the specified port.

section_data.title: Untitled
section_data.summaryData: This code sets up three Express endpoints: a "/api/hello" GET route that responds with a simple greeting message, a "/api/stock/:symbol" GET route that returns either mock or real stock data (including ticker, name, historical daily data, and current price) for a given symbol depending on the USE_MOCK flag, and starts the server listening on the specified port.

data.summary: This code sets up three Express endpoints: a "/api/hello" GET route that responds with a simple greeting message, a "/api/stock/:symbol" GET route that returns either mock or real stock data (including ticker, name, historical daily data, and current price) for a given symbol depending on the USE_MOCK flag, and starts the server listening on the specified port.

The StockChart component receives stock data as a prop, formats it for use with the Recharts library, and renders a responsive line chart displaying the stock's closing prices over time, including labeled axes, a tooltip, and a legend, while showing the company name and ticker above the chart.

data.instruction: according to the stock data, make a red reference line with current price
